title: Siege Engine Range
description: Fire a catapult, trebuchet, or ballista across a medieval range with real per-engine physics, then share or export your best shot.
concept: A side-view artillery range with three distinct siege engines - a torsion catapult, a counterweight trebuchet, and a tension ballista - each with its own tunable parameters and genuinely different velocity/angle physics, so a trebuchet lobs high and far while a ballista fires flat and fast. Adjust arm length, counterweight, draw tension, release angle, wind, and projectile type, then fire at wooden ring targets and castle gates scattered across the range, tracking best distance and hit streaks per engine.
features: 3 siege engine types (catapult, trebuchet, ballista) with distinct physics-driven trajectories
tunable power/angle/wind/projectile sliders with live-updating values
target practice mode with animated trail, hit/miss detection, and per-engine best-distance + streak tracking in localStorage
shareable URL hash (btoa/atob) that restores exact engine + params, plus PNG snapshot export of the landed shot

interaction.mouse: Drag sliders to tune engine parameters, wind, and projectile; click engine chips and projectile buttons to switch loadout; click FIRE to launch.
interaction.click: FIRE launches the current shot; Copy Share Link copies a URL that restores this exact setup; Save Snapshot downloads a PNG of the current trajectory; Reset Range clears hit targets.
interaction.touch: All sliders, engine chips, projectile buttons, and action buttons are large touch-friendly targets usable one-handed on mobile.
interaction.keyboard: Sliders are focusable and adjustable with arrow keys; buttons are focusable and activate with Enter/Space.
